LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS GLOBAL CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC, KEVIN FITZGERALD'S PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. RESPONDENTS DO NOT OPPOSE THE CONFIRMATION OF THE AWARD. PETITIONERS ARE ENTITLED TO PREJUDGMENT INTEREST ON $460,693 FROM NOVEMBER 4, 2016 TO THE DATE OF JUDGMENT TO THE EXTENT THAT THIS AMOUNT HAS NOT BEEN PAID. WHETHER RESPONDENTS ROBERT ZERBST, G. STEPHEN FISHER, AND JULIA LI OLEN HAVE OR HAVE NOT PAID RESPONDENT OVERSEAS INVESTOR SERVICES, LLC THE COST OF DEFENDING THEM IN THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS IS A FACTUAL ISSUE NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON THIS PETITION AND SHOULD BE RAISED WITH THE ARBITRATOR IN THE FIRST INSTANCE, NOT THIS COURT. PETITIONERS ARE AWARDED $749.95 IN COSTS PER CCP 1293.2. RESPONDENTS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO ANY AWARD OF COSTS SINCE THEY ARE NOT THE PREVAILING PARTIES IN THIS CASE AND JUDGE ULMER CHOSE NOT TO AWARD ANY COSTS TO THEM ON THE EARLIER PETITION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS/JUDGEMENT DEBTORS ROBERT ZERBST, G. STEPHEN FISHER, JULIA OLEN and OVERSEAS INVESTOR SERVICES, LLC'S Motion To Compel Acknowledgment Of Partial Satisfaction Of Judgment
is DENIED without prejudice to being refiled after the resolution of judgment creditors Global Capital Advisors, LLC and Kevin Fitzgerald's demand for arbitration filed June 15, 2017. This motion turns on whether the arbitration award requires the judgment debtors to repay OIS for the costs of defending the arbitration in addition to forgiving OIS for the loans made to cover the costs of defending the arbitration. This is an issue that should be resolved by the arbitrator or, at the very least, be deferred until it is determined whether the arbitrator will resolve it. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Notice Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Kirk B. Freeman; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER GLOBAL CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC, KEVIN FITZGERALD's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award is granted. Respondents do not oppose the confirmation of the award. Petitioners are entitled to prejudgment interest on $460,693 from November 4, 2016 to the date of judgment to the extent that this amount has not been paid. Whether respondents Robert Zerbst, G. Stephen Fisher, and Julia Li Olen have or have not paid respondent Overseas Investor Services, LLC the cost of defending them in the arbitration proceedings is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on this petition and should be raised with the arbitrator in the first instance, not this court. Petitioners are awarded $749.95 in costs per CCP 1293.2. Respondents are not entitled to any award of costs since they are not the prevailing parties in this case and Judge Ulmer chose not to award any costs to them on the earlier petition. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Petition To Correct And Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice; Declaration Of Kirk B. Freeman; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KIRK B. FREEMAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-398-1082 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONERS;
ATTY. MARK E. MCKEEN OF DLA PIPER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-615-6096 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT;
ATTY. ROBERT P. TAYLOR OF RPT LEGAL STRATEGIES, PC, TEL. NO. 1-650-400-6992 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS GLOBAL CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC AND KEVIN FITZGERALD'S Petition To Correct And Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award - The petition to correct the arbitration award is DENIED. The arbitrator declined a request to correct her award, and my power to do so is strictly limited by CCP 1286.8. The "evident mistake" of CCP 1286.6(a) is limited to "the description of any person, thing or property referred to in the award" (id.) and petitioner points to no such purported mistake here. Likewise, CCP 1286.6(c) is limited to correcting an arbitration award "imperfect in a matter of form, not affecting the merits of the controversy" and petitioner points to no mere matter of form but rather a theory for $500,000-675,000 in added recovery that petitioner tries to support with a lengthy explanation. The one case cited in petitioner's memo - Severtson v. Williams Construction Co. (1985) 173 Cal.App.3rd 86, 93-94 - holds that any CCP 1286.6 "miscalculation" or "mistake" must be evident on the award's face or so readily apparent that explanation is not necessary. The opinions cited for the first time in petitioner's reply are not contrary to Severtson. No sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru
